summary: Identified critical authentication issue in GoToLinks Next.js app. MongoDB integration works perfectly, but Next.js API routes are inaccessible via public URL, causing all signup/login attempts to fail with 404 errors and preventing dashboard redirects.

critical_bugs.issue: API routes return 404 Not Found when accessed via public URL, but work perfectly on localhost:3000
critical_bugs.impact: Complete authentication system failure - users cannot signup, login, or access dashboard
critical_bugs.technical_details: MongoDB connection works, APIs compiled successfully, but public URL routing configuration doesn't reach Next.js API routes

integration_issues.issue: Form submits correctly but API call fails with 404, shows 'Signup failed' error
Form submits correctly but API call fails with 404, shows 'Login failed' error
integration_issues.user_impact: Users cannot login to existing accounts

integration_issues.issue: Users cannot reach dashboard due to failed authentication - redirected to login instead

passed_tests: Next.js application loads and compiles correctly
Signup API works perfectly on localhost:3000 - creates user, session, profile
Login API works perfectly on localhost:3000 - validates credentials, creates session
Signup form UI loads with all required fields (firstName, lastName, email, password)
Login form UI loads with email and password fields
Form validation and submission mechanics work correctly
Error handling displays properly when API fails
Session token generation and cookie setting work correctly (localhost)

success_percentage.backend: 50% - APIs work locally but fail on public URL
success_percentage.frontend: 80% - UI works, but authentication integration fails

action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix Next.js API route routing configuration for public URL. The APIs work perfectly on localhost:3000 but return 404 on the public preview URL. This is preventing all authentication functionality. Possible fixes: 1) Check Kubernetes ingress rules for API path routing, 2) Verify Next.js deployment configuration, 3) Check if supervisor/nginx proxy configuration routes API calls correctly to port 3000.
